source.title: Intervju tjedna, Filip Šaravanja - predsjednik UBIK-a

summary.title_hr: Intervju tjedna, Filip Šaravanja - predsjednik UBIK-a
summary.abstract_hr: U ovom intervjuu, Filip Šaravanja, predsjednik UBIK-a, raspravlja o krhkosti kripto tržišta, utjecaju manipulacija velikih igrača te ključnim regulatornim okvirima u Europskoj uniji (MiCA) i Sjedinjenim Američkim Državama (Clarity Act). Analizira se kako MiCA transformira centralizirane pružatelje usluga u tradicionalne financijske institucije, izazove koje to donosi za konkurenciju i DeFi sektor, te potencijal Clarity Act-a da pojednostavi poslovanje kripto tvrtki u SAD-u. Razgovor naglašava važnost regulatorne jasnoće za dugoročni rast i institucionalno prihvaćanje kripto sektora.
key_points: Kripto tržište je globalno, mlado i radi 24/7, što uz veliku polugu dovodi do izraženijih kaskadnih efekata i volatilnosti u usporedbi s drugim tržištima.

MiCA regulativa pretvara centralizirane pružatelje kripto usluga (CASP-ove) u tradicionalne financijske igrače, što je skup i težak proces, ali ključan za zaštitu malih ulagatelja i smanjenje sistemskog rizika.
Postoji zabrinutost da MiCA, posebno centralizacija nadzora pod ESMO-om i EBA-om, može ubiti konkurenciju među manjim CASP-ovima i povisiti cijenu usluga, što bi moglo naštetiti malim ulagateljima.
DeFi je u MiCA regulativi ostao nedorečen, s nejasnim definicijama decentralizacije i distribuiranosti, što otežava provedbu i stvara 'anchored territory' za inovacije.
Clarity Act u SAD-u bi mogao pojednostaviti licenciranje kripto tvrtki na federalnoj razini, omogućujući im rad u cijelom SAD-u s jednom licencom, što je značajan 'game changer' za američko tržište.
Regulatorna jasnoća, poput MiCA-e i Clarity Act-a, dugoročno će pozitivno utjecati na kripto sektor jer omogućuje ulazak velikih institucionalnih igrača s ogromnim sumama novca.

Filip Šaravanja preporučuje tvrtkama i developerima da iskoriste 'bear market' za 'buildanje', učenje i razvoj unutar blockchain sektora, naglašavajući ulogu UBIK-a kao mosta između tradicionalnog i kripto sektora.
summary.title_en: Interview of the week, Filip Šaravanja - president of UBIK
summary.abstract_en: In this interview, Filip Šaravanja, president of UBIK, discusses the fragility of the crypto market, the impact of manipulations by large players, and key regulatory frameworks in the European Union (MiCA) and the United States of America (Clarity Act). It is analyzed how MiCA transforms centralized service providers into traditional financial institutions, the challenges this brings for competition and the DeFi sector, and the potential of the Clarity Act to simplify the operations of crypto companies in the USA. The conversation emphasizes the importance of regulatory clarity for the long-term growth and institutional acceptance of the crypto sector.
key_points_en: The crypto market is global, young, and operates 24/7, which, along with high leverage, leads to more pronounced cascading effects and volatility compared to other markets.

MiCA regulation transforms centralized crypto service providers (CASPs) into traditional financial players, which is an expensive and difficult process, but crucial for the protection of small investors and the reduction of systemic risk.

There is concern that MiCA, especially the centralization of supervision under ESMA and EBA, can kill competition among smaller CASPs and raise the price of services, which could harm small investors.
DeFi remained undefined in the MiCA regulation, with unclear definitions of decentralization and distributedness, which complicates implementation and creates an 'anchored territory' for innovation.

The Clarity Act in the US could simplify the licensing of crypto companies at the federal level, allowing them to operate across the entire US with a single license, which is a significant 'game changer' for the American market.

Regulatory clarity, such as MiCA and the Clarity Act, will positively impact the crypto sector in the long term because it enables the entry of large institutional players with enormous sums of money.

Filip Šaravanja recommends to companies and developers to utilize the 'bear market' for 'building', learning, and development within the blockchain sector, emphasizing the role of UBIK as a bridge between the traditional and crypto sectors.
